questions.content:
Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
Cochlear implants – Advantages, disadvantages and factors
Hearing aids {{BLANK}} normal sounds, so they are good for mild hearing loss but not for severe loss. Cochlear implants help some people a lot, but do not work so well for everyone. The brain gets different {{BLANK}} from an implant, so users must relearn how to hear. Cochlear implants are not a cure. Users need ongoing training in {{BLANK}}, lip-reading and sign language. The surgery can damage nerves. It may also destroy any {{BLANK}}; in such cases, those users cannot go back to using a hearing aid. The most important factor for selecting users is the {{BLANK}} of their deafness and also age and previous speaking ability. Finally, the condition of the nerve cells in the cochlea is a factor because {{BLANK}} can damage these cells.
